text.title: typescript-patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Four type-system antipatterns the spec must never produce.

antipattern.symptom: Refactor in one module breaks runtime behaviour in five unrelated modules with no compile errors; bug reports describe "wrong field" or "undefined is not a function".
antipattern.root-cause: An exported function or HTTP handler declares `(body: any) => …`. The `any` propagates through every call site and disables type-checking downstream.
antipattern.fix: Replace with `(body: unknown)` plus Zod parse at the boundary. Spec field types.kind="external-input" MUST have validation in {zod-parse, zod-safe-parse}.

antipattern.symptom: HTTP 500s in production with TypeError ("Cannot read property of undefined"). Stack trace points to a service file, not the controller.
antipattern.root-cause: Code uses `const user = req.body as User`. The compile passes regardless of req.body's actual shape; the shape mismatch surfaces only when downstream code reads a missing field.
antipattern.fix: Parse with UserSchema.parse(req.body) (throws ValidationError on mismatch) or safeParse for explicit handling. Spec validation field MUST be zod-parse / zod-safe-parse / type-guard / assertion-fn.

antipattern.symptom: Sporadic TypeErrors at deep property access in production; cannot reproduce locally because the data happened to be present in dev.
antipattern.root-cause: Code uses `user!.profile!.address!.city`. Every `!` silently asserts non-null; the first actual null deep in the chain throws an unhelpful TypeError.
antipattern.fix: Replace with optional chaining + nullish coalescing (`user?.profile?.address?.city ?? '—'`) OR with an assertion function `assertDefined(user, 'user must be loaded by middleware')`.

antipattern.symptom: API responses pass server-side tests but the client errors with "missing field foo". The OpenAPI schema and the runtime validator disagree.
antipattern.root-cause: Two parallel sources of truth: a manually declared TS interface and a Zod schema. A new field was added to one and not the other.
antipattern.fix: Use z.infer<typeof Schema> for the TS type; the schema is the only source of truth. Spec field representation MUST be "zod-schema" for external-input kinds.
